message.content:
You are the OPEN-SIDE INTEGRATOR for the tamarin-rs sealed relicensing campaign (both-sides by role: you may read everything, including the Haskell). Read /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/PROTOCOL.md and the "Pretty round 5/6/7" context in /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/INTEGRATION_REPORT.md first.

STATE. The sealed pretty unit is COMPLETE for both render targets at cleanroom HEAD 77580ac (/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/pretty-clean/): batch echo byte-green (rounds 1-5, already integrated — signature/rules/lemmas/restrictions/macros/predicates batch routes ADOPTED in the tree), and as of round 7 the WEB rendering mode is at FULL byte parity: 367/367 sections, whole main/message and main/rules pane bodies 82/82 + 82/82 byte-identical composed through the producers skin, live replays 4/4 on never-captured theories. The mechanism: web mode sizes tokens to entity-escaped width (< > = 4, & = 5, " = 6, ' = 5) over the same block models; spans are zero-width markers expanded in a post-pass; batch is byte-frozen. The web panes were the LAST ported-caller hold on the pretty surface (see INTEGRATION_REPORT.md round af332efc: "web panes render width-100 HtmlDoc w/ hl_* spans — a different surface keeping every ported renderer alive").

YOUR TASK, in order:
1. RE-SYNC: vendor the round-6+7 sealed sources into the tree's clean copy (crates/tamarin-theory/src/pretty_clean/ + adapter pretty_clean_adapt.rs), HEADERLESS, following the established mechanical recipe from the earlier pretty integrations (diff sealed workspace vs vendored copy; carry web.rs and the module-level w_text/w_char import changes; keep the existing adapter seams). Keep the sealed workspace itself untouched.
2. ROUTE THE WEB PANES: wire the interactive server's main/message and main/rules pane bodies (currently produced by the ported width-100 HtmlDoc pretty machinery, reachable from crates/tamarin-server handlers via the ported pretty_theory/pretty web paths) through the clean web-mode renderer + the already-integrated producers skin. Follow the existing adapter pattern (producers_adapt.rs shows the routing seam for main/help). Adopt ONLY where byte-green; keep-and-report anything that diverges (with a first-diverging-byte witness).
3. VERIFY on the corpus ladder (memory policy: guards → family files → residual set; full web sweep only if the deletion below actually lands): after building, run the pane-byte check against scripts/.web_hs_cache for main/message + main/rules across ALL cached files, then scripts/web_parity.sh, scripts/wf_gate.sh (expect 419 MATCH / 0 DIFF, no regression), scripts/pretty_gate.sh (expect exactly the 403/16 baseline), and the cargo test suites. Oracle scripts carry OOM guards (oom_score_adj/ulimit) — preserve them; do not raise parallelism; maude/dot live in linuxbrew (see scripts for PATH handling). Beware stale binaries — rebuild before gating.
4. THE DELETION ATTEMPT (the point of this round): with both batch and web routes clean, re-attempt dead-code elimination of the ported pretty surface — crates/tamarin-theory/src/pretty_theory.rs and pretty_formula.rs and any other ported pretty file whose last callers were the web panes. Known entanglement from earlier rounds: pretty_theory.rs hosts wf_headerless_preamble registrations and possibly other non-pretty seams — RELOCATE such seams (mechanically, preserving behavior) rather than treating them as blockers, but verify wf_gate stays 419/0 after relocation. Delete only what the compiler + gates prove dead; per-file, if a caller remains, name the exact remaining caller chain in the report (per-unit blockers, not a vague NET-ZERO).
5. HEADERS + AUTHOR DELTA: after deletions, regenerate license headers (scripts/gen_license_headers.py — run its --check mode first to see the current 133 baseline, then regenerate) and report the file-count delta AND the per-author citation delta (which upstream authors' citations disappeared, which remain and via which files). This is the campaign's first potential author-list movement from the pretty cluster — measure it precisely.
6. If ALL gates are green AND deletions landed, run the FULL web sweep (milestone standard) and report its family breakdown vs the 2026-07-07 baseline.

RULES: Do NOT commit or push ANYTHING in /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs (the user commits that repo; leave the working tree building and gated-green). Do not revert or clobber the extensive existing uncommitted work — build on top of it. Comments describe current code only — no narration about what changed or why it is correct. Append a "Pretty round 7 integration" section to /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/INTEGRATION_REPORT.md (do not commit the cleanroom repo either — the orchestrator commits it). Fix issues you find as you go; your report states what IS, verified by the gates, not intentions.
Return a compact summary: what was routed/adopted, what was deleted (file list), header count before/after, per-author delta, every gate's exact result, and any keep-and-report residues with witnesses.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing

Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.

## Rooms
- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
  as evidence of non-access.

## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
   boundary signatures only).
2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
   observation INPUTS.
3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.

## Forbidden to the clean room
- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.

## Audit & integration (after implementation)
1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
   forbidden path was accessed.
2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
3. Behavioral acceptance: the FULL-CORPUS gate for the slice must pass, not a
   fixture subset (see "Full-corpus gates" below). A narrow fixture suite that
   passes while the corpus gate fails is not acceptance.
4. Integration: the open side may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
   with them (history retains them under GPL). Every integrator that touches a
   slice with a full-corpus gate MUST run that gate before declaring green.

## Full-corpus gates (standard, not optional)

Each slice has a fast oracle gate over the whole 419-file example corpus,
diffing the slice's output against the Haskell reference cache. These are the
sealed implementer's primary data source (run between build and the HS cache
every iteration) AND the integrator's mandatory acceptance check. Both take
`RS_PATH` (point at any build) and `ALLOWLIST` (subset while iterating).

- Wellformedness: `scripts/wf_gate.sh` — runs the binary WITHOUT `--prove`
  (the wf block prints at theory-load, ~1s/file; full corpus ~72s at JOBS=6),
  extracts the `/* WARNING … */` block, diffs vs `scripts/.hs_file_cache`.
- Web: `scripts/web_parity.sh` — crawls HS and RS interactive servers per
  theory, semantic-diffs the response manifests vs `scripts/.web_hs_cache`.
- Full batch (proof output, slow ~30-60min): `scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh` —
  the ground-truth `--prove` byte diff; run at milestones, not every iteration.

Once a slice's ported code is DELETED, the integrated module IS the sealed
reimplementation, so running the gate against the tree observes the sealed
code's own output vs the reference — legitimate feedback, not observation of
replaced code. For a not-yet-integrated slice the tree still holds the ported
code, so gate only the workspace build (via `RS_PATH`), never the stock tree.

4482	# Open-side integration report — pretty round-3+4 (ENDGAME: rules + lemmas)
4483	
4484	Date: 2026-07-19. Integrator: open side (mechanical re-sync + value adapters +
4485	a ported sort-resolution pre-pass; no logic transplanted INTO the clean crate).
4486	Repo: `/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs`. Builds on "pretty round-1/round-2" above.
4487	Sealed rounds 3+4 delivered R3 (formula/lemma/restriction) and the R4 blocker
4488	fixes (SAPIC `process=` attribute, tuple/application `>)` own-line wrap, and the
4489	STACK-OVERFLOW fix via a NEW iterative `doc.rs` engine). Outcome: the **rule**
4490	and **lemma** batch-echo routes are ADOPTED (byte-green); **restriction**,
4491	**macros**, the **theory-frame** assembly, and the **web signature pane** are
4492	kept ported with concrete sealed-side witnesses; and DELETION is **NET-ZERO**
4493	— the interactive web panes retain a live caller on every ported renderer.
4494	
4495	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4496	## 0. Re-sync of the round-3+4 clean sources — DONE (headerless)
4497	
4498	Re-applied the round-1/2 mechanical recipe (`crate::` -> `super::`; `lib.rs` ->
4499	`mod.rs`) from `pretty/workspace/pretty-clean/src` into
4500	`crates/tamarin-theory/src/pretty_clean/`. Reverse-transform byte-identity
4501	(`sed 's/super::/crate::/g'`) verified for all nine `crate::`-carrying files;
4502	`doc.rs` byte-identical verbatim (its pre-existing `use super::*;` test import
4503	means it carries no `crate::` — the round-1 exception). CHANGED by R3+R4:
4504	`ast.rs` (R3 formula/atom/lemma/restriction/guarded types), `formula.rs` (NEW),
4505	`lemma.rs` (NEW), `doc.rs` (R4 iterative engine, 28.9 KB -> 36.7 KB),
4506	`rule.rs`/`term.rs` (R4 wrap fixes), `mod.rs` (R3 render entry points). Only
4507	`.hs` citation across the tree is `HughesPJ.hs` in `doc.rs` (EXTERNAL skip-set);
4508	0 clean files headered. Pretty gate after re-sync alone: **403 MATCH / 16 DIFF**
4509	(unchanged — the new iterative `doc.rs` reproduces the signature block exactly).
4510	
4511	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4512	## 1. Adapters — headerless, workspace-authored (value translation only)
4513	
4514	`pretty_clean_adapt.rs` gained: `fact`/`rule_attr` (1:1 structural, color
4515	`#`-strip+lowercase), `rule_section` + `AcVariantsInput` (builds the clean
4516	`Rule`+`AcVariants` from the ported closure DATA), `formula`/`atom` (1:1),
4517	`lemma_section` + `GuardedInput` + `lemma_attr`/`trace_quantifier`. Every render
4518	byte comes out of the clean crate; all transforms stay ported.
4519	
4520	A ported sort-resolution pre-pass **`pretty_formula::resolve_formula_sorts`**
4521	(new, headered) was required: the clean formula renderer is sort-LITERAL (it
4522	prints sigils from `VarSpec.sort`), whereas the ported printer resolves a
4523	reference's sort from its binder via scope (timepoints -> Node, Untagged msg
4524	refs -> binder sort) and collapses consecutive same-kind quantifiers. The
4525	pre-pass reuses the EXISTING scope machinery (`open_formula_prefix`,
4526	`allocate_formula_binders_refs`, `resolved_sort_pos`, `lookup_display`,
4527	`PreciseFreshState`) and emits a resolved `p::Formula`. Without it every
4528	restriction/lemma `@ tp` printed `tp` instead of `#tp` (206 DIFFs -> after the
4529	pre-pass, 0 except the macro witnesses below).
4530	
4531	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4532	## 2. RULE ROUTE (2a) — ADOPTED (byte-green; R4 closed all three R2 witnesses)
4533	
4534	`render_rule`'s BATCH assembly now builds the clean `Rule` (modulo E) +
4535	`Option<AcVariants>` from the ported DATA (desugared + arity1 + AC-canonicalised
4536	E facts; abstracted AC rule; residual `variant_substs`; gated outer + in-comment
4537	loop-breaker indices) and calls `pretty_clean::render_rule`. Gate
4538	(`pretty_gate_final_1.tsv`): **403 MATCH / 16 DIFF**, ZERO new divergence — the
4539	round-2 rule route's 88 DIFFs (R3-a `process=`, R3-b tuple/app wrap, R3-c stack
4540	overflow) are ALL closed. Verified MATCH: `accountability/csf21-acc-unbounded/
4541	previous/ct.spthy` (process=), all five `ake/dh/{UM_three_pass*,DHKEA_NAXOS_C_
4542	eCK_PFS*_partially_matching}.spthy` (tuple wrap), and the four previously-
4543	crashing deep files `fm24-cardpayments/onlineAuthorized/C8.spthy`, `idbased/
4544	BP_IBS_{2,3,4}.spthy`.
4545	
4546	## 3. LEMMA ROUTE (2b) — ADOPTED (byte-green)
4547	
4548	`render_parsed_lemma`'s BATCH assembly routes through `pretty_clean::
4549	render_lemma`: header (`lemma name [attrs]:`), the `all-traces|exists-trace
4550	"formula"` statement (clean formula renderer over the sort-resolved statement
4551	formula), the guarded-formula comment FRAME (guarded text supplied opaque from
4552	the ported `pretty_guarded_doublequoted` + `gnot`), and the proof tail. The
4553	`heuristic=` value is fed ALREADY oracle-expanded (file-dependent
4554	`prettyGoalRankings` stays ported). Gate (`pretty_gate_final_2.tsv`): **403
4555	MATCH / 16 DIFF**, ZERO new divergence. `--prove` byte spot-check
4556	(`corpus_file_diff.sh`): `Tutorial` 250/250 MATCH, `ake/dh/UM_three_pass`
4557	13899/13899 MATCH, `features/private_function_symbols/NAXOS_eCK_PFS_private`
4558	316/316 MATCH — the proof-tail framing is byte-faithful under `--prove` too.
4559	
4560	## 4. RESTRICTION (2b) — BLOCKED, REVERTED (1 witness)
4561	
4562	The clean `Restriction` carries a SINGLE `formula` and `restriction_doc` renders
4563	it for BOTH the top-line statement AND the `/* expanded formula: … */` comment.
4564	HS shows the ORIGINAL (macro-form) on top and the macro-EXPANDED formula in the
4565	comment, which differ under macros: `features/macros/MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions
4566	.spthy` — top `A( m(m3(x)) )` vs comment `A( x )` — gated 402/17. Reverted.
4567	**Sealed-side target:** the `Restriction` type needs a separate expanded-formula
4568	field. Every non-macro restriction (401) rendered byte-green via the clean route
4569	before the revert, so the blocker is exactly the single-formula limitation.
4570	
4571	## 5. MACROS (2c) — BLOCKED, REVERTED (1 witness)
4572	
4573	The clean `macros::macros_doc` joins the block with `sep` (ONE line when it fits
4574	the width), but HS uses `keyword_ "macros:" $$ nest 4 (vcat …)`: the first macro
4575	sits beside `macros: ` and EVERY subsequent macro drops to its own 8-indented
4576	line regardless of fit. Witness `MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions.spthy` (three short
4577	macros that fit but HS still stacks) — gated 402/17. Reverted. **Sealed-side
4578	target:** `macros_doc` must use the `$$`/vcat layout, not `sep`.
4579	
4580	## 6. THEORY-FRAME assembly (2d) — BLOCKED
4581	
4582	Clean `theory::render` is still `unimplemented!()` (the top-level assembly was
4583	never sealed), so the echo frame/section-ordering cannot be routed. Not attempted.
4584	
4585	## 7. WEB SIGNATURE PANE (3) — BLOCKED
4586	
4587	Not routable: the clean `render_signature_block` is PLAIN-TEXT, FIXED-WIDTH
4588	(110/73), and emits the `// Function signature …` header; the web message pane
4589	renders under `HtmlDocGuard` (entity escaping + `hl_*` highlight spans) at
4590	`WEB_LINE_LENGTH=100` with NO header. `render_signature`/`web_signature_block`
4591	stay ported. **Sealed-side target:** an HtmlDoc-capable, width-parameterised
4592	clean signature API.
4593	
4594	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4595	## 8. WIDTH/HTML discovery + the batch-width gate (load-bearing)
4596	
4597	The clean crate hardcodes width 110/73 and is plain-text. The interactive server
4598	calls `set_display_width(100, 67)` process-wide and renders the message/rules
4599	pane under `HtmlDocGuard`. So the clean routes are correct ONLY at the batch echo
4600	width. First adoption broke `web_parity` (2 Tutorial DIFFs: the source-view lemma
4601	wrapped at 110 not 100). Fix: added `pretty_hpj::display_line_length()` (getter)
4602	and gated `render_rule` + `render_parsed_lemma` on `batch_echo_width()` (==110)
4603	— batch echo -> clean; the web source view (width 100) and message/rules pane
4604	(HtmlDoc) -> the ported width-/HTML-aware printers (`render_parsed_lemma_ported`,
4605	`render_rule`'s ported branch). `web_parity` restored to **158/0**.
4606	
4607	--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4608	## 9. DELETION ANALYSIS — NET-ZERO (per-unit blocker: the web panes stay ported)
4609	
4610	With the rule + lemma batch routes live, the compiler reports **0 dead
4611	functions** in `pretty_theory.rs` / `pretty_formula.rs` (`cargo build` warnings
4612	= none; call-graph grep confirms). Reason: `render_rule` and
4613	`render_parsed_lemma` are reached at BOTH the batch width (CLI echo -> clean) AND
4614	the web width (interactive `/thy/trace/#/source` + `/message` source view ->
4615	ported fallback), and the web message/rules pane (`web_proto_rules` ->
4616	`render_rule`, under `HtmlDocGuard`) is HTML-mode — so every ported renderer
4617	(`render_rule_body`, `render_ac_variants_block`, `rule_attributes_doc`,
4618	`render_variant_substs_block`, `variant_subst_doc`, `render_loop_breakers_line`,
4619	`lemma_attr_docs`, `render_guarded_block`, `quantifier_keyword`, and
4620	`pretty_formula::lemma_header_line`/`formula_to_doc`/`pp_*`) retains a LIVE web
4621	caller. The clean route ADDS the batch path; it does not remove the web path.
4622	
4623	* Functions deleted: **0** (LOC deleted: **0**).
4624	* Header delta: **0** (133 -> 133 GPL-headered `.rs`; `gen_license_headers.py`
4625	  regenerate updated 0 files; `--check` = 0 stale).
4626	* Per-author delta on `pretty_theory.rs`: **0** — all ~25 authors remain (the
4627	  web message/source panes keep `render_rule` + helpers and
4628	  `render_parsed_lemma_ported` + helpers alive via surviving cited ranges
4629	  `ClosedTheory.hs`, `OpenTheory.hs`, `Rule.hs`, `Lemma.hs`, `SubstVFresh.hs`,
4630	  `TheoryObject.hs`).
4631	* Per-author delta on `pretty_formula.rs`: **0** — all 9 authors (meiersi,
4632	  beschmi, jdreier, PhilipLukertWork, rkunnema, rsasse, BTom-GH, charlie-j, arcz)
4633	  remain (the ported lemma statement, restriction, guarded, and
4634	  intruder-variant paths keep `Formula.hs`/`Guarded.hs`/`Atom.hs`/`Term.hs`

4636	
4637	This matches the campaign's documented NET-ZERO pattern: the batch echo now
4638	routes rule + lemma + signature through the sealed clean crate byte-identically,

# Open-side integration report — pretty round-3+4 (ENDGAME: rules + lemmas)

Date: 2026-07-19. Integrator: open side (mechanical re-sync + value adapters +
a ported sort-resolution pre-pass; no logic transplanted INTO the clean crate).
Repo: `/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs`. Builds on "pretty round-1/round-2" above.
Sealed rounds 3+4 delivered R3 (formula/lemma/restriction) and the R4 blocker
fixes (SAPIC `process=` attribute, tuple/application `>)` own-line wrap, and the
STACK-OVERFLOW fix via a NEW iterative `doc.rs` engine). Outcome: the **rule**
and **lemma** batch-echo routes are ADOPTED (byte-green); **restriction**,
**macros**, the **theory-frame** assembly, and the **web signature pane** are
kept ported with concrete sealed-side witnesses; and DELETION is **NET-ZERO**
— the interactive web panes retain a live caller on every ported renderer.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 0. Re-sync of the round-3+4 clean sources — DONE (headerless)

Re-applied the round-1/2 mechanical recipe (`crate::` -> `super::`; `lib.rs` ->
`mod.rs`) from `pretty/workspace/pretty-clean/src` into
`crates/tamarin-theory/src/pretty_clean/`. Reverse-transform byte-identity
(`sed 's/super::/crate::/g'`) verified for all nine `crate::`-carrying files;
`doc.rs` byte-identical verbatim (its pre-existing `use super::*;` test import
means it carries no `crate::` — the round-1 exception). CHANGED by R3+R4:
`ast.rs` (R3 formula/atom/lemma/restriction/guarded types), `formula.rs` (NEW),
`lemma.rs` (NEW), `doc.rs` (R4 iterative engine, 28.9 KB -> 36.7 KB),
`rule.rs`/`term.rs` (R4 wrap fixes), `mod.rs` (R3 render entry points). Only
`.hs` citation across the tree is `HughesPJ.hs` in `doc.rs` (EXTERNAL skip-set);
0 clean files headered. Pretty gate after re-sync alone: **403 MATCH / 16 DIFF**
(unchanged — the new iterative `doc.rs` reproduces the signature block exactly).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 1. Adapters — headerless, workspace-authored (value translation only)

`pretty_clean_adapt.rs` gained: `fact`/`rule_attr` (1:1 structural, color
`#`-strip+lowercase), `rule_section` + `AcVariantsInput` (builds the clean
`Rule`+`AcVariants` from the ported closure DATA), `formula`/`atom` (1:1),
`lemma_section` + `GuardedInput` + `lemma_attr`/`trace_quantifier`. Every render
byte comes out of the clean crate; all transforms stay ported.

A ported sort-resolution pre-pass **`pretty_formula::resolve_formula_sorts`**
(new, headered) was required: the clean formula renderer is sort-LITERAL (it
prints sigils from `VarSpec.sort`), whereas the ported printer resolves a
reference's sort from its binder via scope (timepoints -> Node, Untagged msg
refs -> binder sort) and collapses consecutive same-kind quantifiers. The
pre-pass reuses the EXISTING scope machinery (`open_formula_prefix`,
`allocate_formula_binders_refs`, `resolved_sort_pos`, `lookup_display`,
`PreciseFreshState`) and emits a resolved `p::Formula`. Without it every
restriction/lemma `@ tp` printed `tp` instead of `#tp` (206 DIFFs -> after the
pre-pass, 0 except the macro witnesses below).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 2. RULE ROUTE (2a) — ADOPTED (byte-green; R4 closed all three R2 witnesses)

`render_rule`'s BATCH assembly now builds the clean `Rule` (modulo E) +
`Option<AcVariants>` from the ported DATA (desugared + arity1 + AC-canonicalised
E facts; abstracted AC rule; residual `variant_substs`; gated outer + in-comment
loop-breaker indices) and calls `pretty_clean::render_rule`. Gate
(`pretty_gate_final_1.tsv`): **403 MATCH / 16 DIFF**, ZERO new divergence — the
round-2 rule route's 88 DIFFs (R3-a `process=`, R3-b tuple/app wrap, R3-c stack
overflow) are ALL closed. Verified MATCH: `accountability/csf21-acc-unbounded/
previous/ct.spthy` (process=), all five `ake/dh/{UM_three_pass*,DHKEA_NAXOS_C_
eCK_PFS*_partially_matching}.spthy` (tuple wrap), and the four previously-
crashing deep files `fm24-cardpayments/onlineAuthorized/C8.spthy`, `idbased/
BP_IBS_{2,3,4}.spthy`.

## 3. LEMMA ROUTE (2b) — ADOPTED (byte-green)

`render_parsed_lemma`'s BATCH assembly routes through `pretty_clean::
render_lemma`: header (`lemma name [attrs]:`), the `all-traces|exists-trace
"formula"` statement (clean formula renderer over the sort-resolved statement
formula), the guarded-formula comment FRAME (guarded text supplied opaque from
the ported `pretty_guarded_doublequoted` + `gnot`), and the proof tail. The
`heuristic=` value is fed ALREADY oracle-expanded (file-dependent
`prettyGoalRankings` stays ported). Gate (`pretty_gate_final_2.tsv`): **403
MATCH / 16 DIFF**, ZERO new divergence. `--prove` byte spot-check
(`corpus_file_diff.sh`): `Tutorial` 250/250 MATCH, `ake/dh/UM_three_pass`
13899/13899 MATCH, `features/private_function_symbols/NAXOS_eCK_PFS_private`
316/316 MATCH — the proof-tail framing is byte-faithful under `--prove` too.

## 4. RESTRICTION (2b) — BLOCKED, REVERTED (1 witness)

The clean `Restriction` carries a SINGLE `formula` and `restriction_doc` renders
it for BOTH the top-line statement AND the `/* expanded formula: … */` comment.
HS shows the ORIGINAL (macro-form) on top and the macro-EXPANDED formula in the
comment, which differ under macros: `features/macros/MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions
.spthy` — top `A( m(m3(x)) )` vs comment `A( x )` — gated 402/17. Reverted.
**Sealed-side target:** the `Restriction` type needs a separate expanded-formula
field. Every non-macro restriction (401) rendered byte-green via the clean route
before the revert, so the blocker is exactly the single-formula limitation.

## 5. MACROS (2c) — BLOCKED, REVERTED (1 witness)

The clean `macros::macros_doc` joins the block with `sep` (ONE line when it fits
the width), but HS uses `keyword_ "macros:" $$ nest 4 (vcat …)`: the first macro
sits beside `macros: ` and EVERY subsequent macro drops to its own 8-indented
line regardless of fit. Witness `MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions.spthy` (three short
macros that fit but HS still stacks) — gated 402/17. Reverted. **Sealed-side
target:** `macros_doc` must use the `$$`/vcat layout, not `sep`.

## 6. THEORY-FRAME assembly (2d) — BLOCKED

Clean `theory::render` is still `unimplemented!()` (the top-level assembly was
never sealed), so the echo frame/section-ordering cannot be routed. Not attempted.

## 7. WEB SIGNATURE PANE (3) — BLOCKED

Not routable: the clean `render_signature_block` is PLAIN-TEXT, FIXED-WIDTH
(110/73), and emits the `// Function signature …` header; the web message pane
renders under `HtmlDocGuard` (entity escaping + `hl_*` highlight spans) at
`WEB_LINE_LENGTH=100` with NO header. `render_signature`/`web_signature_block`
stay ported. **Sealed-side target:** an HtmlDoc-capable, width-parameterised
clean signature API.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 8. WIDTH/HTML discovery + the batch-width gate (load-bearing)

The clean crate hardcodes width 110/73 and is plain-text. The interactive server
calls `set_display_width(100, 67)` process-wide and renders the message/rules
pane under `HtmlDocGuard`. So the clean routes are correct ONLY at the batch echo
width. First adoption broke `web_parity` (2 Tutorial DIFFs: the source-view lemma
wrapped at 110 not 100). Fix: added `pretty_hpj::display_line_length()` (getter)
and gated `render_rule` + `render_parsed_lemma` on `batch_echo_width()` (==110)
— batch echo -> clean; the web source view (width 100) and message/rules pane
(HtmlDoc) -> the ported width-/HTML-aware printers (`render_parsed_lemma_ported`,
`render_rule`'s ported branch). `web_parity` restored to **158/0**.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 9. DELETION ANALYSIS — NET-ZERO (per-unit blocker: the web panes stay ported)

With the rule + lemma batch routes live, the compiler reports **0 dead
functions** in `pretty_theory.rs` / `pretty_formula.rs` (`cargo build` warnings
= none; call-graph grep confirms). Reason: `render_rule` and
`render_parsed_lemma` are reached at BOTH the batch width (CLI echo -> clean) AND
the web width (interactive `/thy/trace/#/source` + `/message` source view ->
ported fallback), and the web message/rules pane (`web_proto_rules` ->
`render_rule`, under `HtmlDocGuard`) is HTML-mode — so every ported renderer
(`render_rule_body`, `render_ac_variants_block`, `rule_attributes_doc`,
`render_variant_substs_block`, `variant_subst_doc`, `render_loop_breakers_line`,
`lemma_attr_docs`, `render_guarded_block`, `quantifier_keyword`, and
`pretty_formula::lemma_header_line`/`formula_to_doc`/`pp_*`) retains a LIVE web
caller. The clean route ADDS the batch path; it does not remove the web path.

* Functions deleted: **0** (LOC deleted: **0**).
* Header delta: **0** (133 -> 133 GPL-headered `.rs`; `gen_license_headers.py`
  regenerate updated 0 files; `--check` = 0 stale).
* Per-author delta on `pretty_theory.rs`: **0** — all ~25 authors remain (the
  web message/source panes keep `render_rule` + helpers and
  `render_parsed_lemma_ported` + helpers alive via surviving cited ranges
  `ClosedTheory.hs`, `OpenTheory.hs`, `Rule.hs`, `Lemma.hs`, `SubstVFresh.hs`,
  `TheoryObject.hs`).
* Per-author delta on `pretty_formula.rs`: **0** — all 9 authors (meiersi,
  beschmi, jdreier, PhilipLukertWork, rkunnema, rsasse, BTom-GH, charlie-j, arcz)
  remain (the ported lemma statement, restriction, guarded, and
  intruder-variant paths keep `Formula.hs`/`Guarded.hs`/`Atom.hs`/`Term.hs`
  cited ranges live).

This matches the campaign's documented NET-ZERO pattern: the batch echo now
routes rule + lemma + signature through the sealed clean crate byte-identically,
but deletion is gated on a future round clean-rooming the WEB layer (a
width-parameterised, HtmlDoc-capable clean renderer) so the web callers drop.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 10. Full gates — all green

* `cargo build --release` — 0 errors.
* `cargo test --workspace` — all suites pass, 0 failed; `console_split_parity`
  **59 passed / 0 failed**.
* pretty_gate (`pretty_gate_final.tsv`, JOBS=6) — **403 MATCH / 16 DIFF / 0
  SKIP** (the 16 = `features/auto-sources/spore/*` closure gap, unchanged).
* wf_gate (`wf_gate_after_pretty_final.tsv`, JOBS=6) — **419 MATCH / 0 DIFF**,
  rows `diff`-identical to `scripts/wf_gate_round7.tsv`.
* web_parity (`web_parity_final.tsv`, Tutorial seed) — **158 MATCH / 0 DIFF**.
* `--prove` byte spot-check (`corpus_file_diff.sh`) — Tutorial / UM_three_pass /
  NAXOS_eCK_PFS_private all MATCH (rule + lemma routes byte-faithful under prove).
* `gen_license_headers.py` + `--check` — **0 stale**, **133** headered, delta 0.

Deleted: none. **Header delta: 0 (133 -> 133).** Adopted: rule + lemma batch
routes (byte-green). Kept ported with witnesses: restriction, macros,
theory-frame, web signature. Blocker to deletion: the interactive web panes.

################################################################################

################################################################################

via the unchanged solver transforms, then the batch path sort-resolves both and
hands them to `pretty_clean_adapt::restriction_section`. The clean
`Restriction.expanded` field carries the R3+4 single-formula blocker away — the
statement now renders `original`, the `/* expanded formula: … */` comment
renders `expanded`. Gate (`pretty_gate_r5_restr_mac_pred.tsv`): **403 MATCH /
16 DIFF**, ZERO new divergence. The round-3+4 revert witness
`features/macros/MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions.spthy` (statement `A( m(m3(x)) )`
vs comment `A( x )`) is now MATCH, and all 401 non-macro restrictions (where
`expanded == original`) stayed byte-green. The clean `formula::is_safety`
classifier (exercised on every restriction for the `// safety formula` line)
agrees with the ported `is_safety_formula` across the whole corpus.

## 3. MACROS ROUTE (2b) — ADOPTED (byte-green; R3+4 blocker closed)

`render_parsed_macros` gained a `batch_echo_width()` gate routing the block
through `pretty_clean_adapt::macros_section`. The clean `macros_doc` now uses
the always-break `vcat` layout (first macro beside `macros: `, every subsequent
macro on its own 8-indented line regardless of fit) — the exact `sep`->`vcat`
fix the R3+4 witness demanded. Same gate run: **403 MATCH / 16 DIFF**, ZERO new
divergence. `MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions.spthy` (three short macros that fit
one line but HS stacks) is MATCH.

## 4. PREDICATES ROUTE (2c) — ADOPTED (byte-green)

The `Predicates` item branch in `render_parsed_item` gained a
`batch_echo_width()` gate: it extracts each predicate's formal params (always
plain sorted variables — parsed via `fact()`), arity-1-folds and sort-resolves
the body, and routes the run through `pretty_clean_adapt::predicates_section`
-> clean `render_predicates` (the `predicate: <fact><=><formula>` per predicate,
blank-line separated, with the margin-0 body splice). A defensive fallback keeps
the ported per-predicate join for any (corpus-absent) predicate whose head is
persistent/annotated or not a plain var-parametrised fact. Same gate run:
**403 MATCH / 16 DIFF**, ZERO new divergence. The sealed margin-0 splice
witnesses are MATCH: `accountability/.../mixnets/basic/dmn-basic.spthy` (two
different-length bodies both wrapping at column 1),
`features/predicates/minimal.spthy`, `sapic/fast/feature-predicates/timepoints
.spthy`.

## 5. THEORY-FRAME assembly (2d) — DECLINED (concrete reason; block-level is the
##     end state)

Not adopted. The clean `theory::render` models the GATE-STRIPPED echo:
`parts.join("\n\n") + "\n\n\n\nend"`, deliberately omitting the trailing
wellformedness report and `Generated from:` stamp (their blank-line residue is
reproduced) and never modeling the `configuration:` line, the tactics block, the
theory-level `heuristic:` pre-items block, or the injective-fact-insts `ppCache`
comment. The real CLI echo (`render_theory_echo`) must EMIT all of those before
`end` (and `wf_gate` reads the wf block), so wiring `render_theory` would drop
content the product requires. Partial block-level routing — signature + rules +
lemmas + restrictions + macros + predicates all through the clean crate
byte-identically — is the accepted end state (task step 2d permits it). The
`theory::render` re-sync is carried for provenance but has no call site.

## 6. WEB SIGNATURE PANE (3) — still BLOCKED (unchanged from R3+4)

Not routable: the clean signature block is plain-text, fixed-width, header-
carrying; the web message pane is HtmlDoc, width-100, header-less. Unchanged.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 7. WIDTH gate (load-bearing, unchanged)

All three new routes are gated on `batch_echo_width()` (== display width 110),
same as the R3+4 rule/lemma routes: batch echo -> clean; the interactive web
source view (width 100) and message/rules panes (HtmlDoc) -> the ported
width-/HTML-aware printers. This is what keeps `web_parity` green (the "batch
routes only" rule) — the web panes hold the ported files.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
## 8. DELETION ANALYSIS — NET-ZERO (unchanged pattern; do-not-repeat per plan)

With restriction + macros + predicates now ALSO batch-routed, the compiler still
reports 0 dead functions: `render_parsed_restriction` (HtmlDoc web message pane
+ web source view), `render_parsed_macros` (`web_macros` rules pane), and
`render_predicate` (web source view) each retain a live non-batch caller via the
`batch_echo_width()` fallback branch. The clean route ADDS the batch path; it
does not remove the web path.

content.text: The mechanism is clear: rounds 6+7 added `web.rs` (the `w_text`/`w_char` size-aware producers + `hl_*` span emitters + web render entry points) and rewired formula/lemma/macros/term/rule/signature to import `w_char as char`, `w_text as text` from `web` and emit `hl_*` spans. Let me verify the transform faithfulness before vendoring.

input.command:
SEALED=/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/pretty-clean/src
SCRATCH=/tmp/claude-1000/-home-kamilner-tamarin-rs/57f8e1bc-d495-4b36-93c0-033347d5034a/scratchpad
echo "=== Verify: diff(sealed, vendored) touches ONLY crate::/super:: lines (empty = only transform lines) ==="
for f in $SEALED/*.rs; do
  base=$(basename $f)
  out=$base; [ "$base" = "lib.rs" ] && out=mod.rs
  # lines that differ but do NOT contain crate:: or super:: -> should be none
  bad=$(diff "$f" "$SCRATCH/vendored/$out" | grep '^[<>]' | grep -v 'crate::\|super::' | grep -v '^[<>] *$')
  if [ -n "$bad" ]; then echo "!!! $base has NON-transform diffs:"; echo "$bad"; else echo "OK   $base (only crate::/super:: lines differ)"; fi
done
echo ""
echo "=== Reverse-transform byte-identity (super::->crate:: == sealed) for pure-crate:: files ==="
for base in ast formula lemma macros rule signature term theory; do
  out=$base
  if diff -q <(sed 's/super::/crate::/g' "$SCRATCH/vendored/$out.rs") "$SEALED/$out.rs" >/dev/null; then echo "IDENTICAL(reverse) $out.rs"; else echo "MISMATCH $out.rs"; fi
done
echo "=== lib.rs->mod.rs reverse check ==="
diff -q <(sed 's/super::/crate::/g' "$SCRATCH/vendored/mod.rs") "$SEALED/lib.rs" >/dev/null && echo "IDENTICAL(reverse) mod.rs<-lib.rs" || echo "MISMATCH mod.rs"
echo "=== doc.rs verbatim (0 crate::, pre-existing super::) ==="
diff -q "$SEALED/doc.rs" "$SCRATCH/vendored/doc.rs" >/dev/null && echo "IDENTICAL(verbatim) doc.rs"
echo "=== web.rs: only crate::->super:: lines differ from sealed (test import super::* untouched) ==="
diff "$SEALED/web.rs" "$SCRATCH/vendored/web.rs"

1	//! pretty-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover theory
2	//! pretty-printer (the `theory <name> begin … end` echo), derived purely from
3	//! black-box oracle behavior. See ../../SPEC.md for the observable boundary and
4	//! the R1–R4 sub-target decomposition, and workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the
5	//! inferred behavioral spec you build up as you probe the oracle.
6	//!
7	//! Implemented sub-targets: R1 (term core + signature block), R2 (rule
8	//! blocks + macros surface), R3 (restriction / lemma / formula blocks), R4
9	//! (macros / predicates blocks), and R5 — the top-level `theory <name> begin
10	//! … end` frame (`theory::render`), the restriction statement/expanded-formula
11	//! split, and the always-break macros layout.
12	
13	pub mod ast;
14	pub mod doc; // BSD HughesPJ Doc engine — reuse graphdot's clean-room port.
15	
16	pub mod formula; // R3
17	pub mod lemma; // R3
18	pub mod macros; // R4
19	pub mod rule; // R2
20	pub mod signature; // R1
21	pub mod term; // R1 (deep core)
22	pub mod theory; // top-level assembly
23	pub mod web; // R6 — the interactive server's HTML rendering mode
24	
25	pub use ast::*;
26	
27	/// Whole theory echo: `theory <name> begin … end`, minus the wf comment and
28	/// the Generated-from stamp (both appended by the caller / stripped by the
29	/// gate). Top-level assembly of the R1–R4 sub-targets. See `theory::render`.
30	pub fn render_theory(thy: &ast::Theory, sig: &ast::Signature) -> String {
31	    theory::render(thy, sig)
32	}
33	
34	/// R1: the signature section — header comment, blank line, then the
35	/// `builtins:` / `functions:` / `equations:` declarations. See
36	/// `signature::render`.
37	pub fn render_signature_block(sig: &ast::Signature) -> String {
38	    signature::render(sig)
39	}
40	
41	/// R1: one term, rendered at the echo's layout parameters. See `term::render`.
42	pub fn render_term(term: &ast::Term) -> String {
43	    term::render(term)
44	}
45	
46	/// R4: the `macros:` block. See `macros::render_macros`.
47	pub fn render_macros(macros: &[ast::Macro]) -> String {
48	    macros::render_macros(macros)
49	}
50	
51	/// R4: a contiguous run of `predicate:` declarations. See
52	/// `macros::render_predicates`.
53	pub fn render_predicates(preds: &[ast::Predicate]) -> String {
54	    macros::render_predicates(preds)
55	}
56	
57	/// R2: one whole rule block — header, body, blank line, loop-breaker line,
58	/// and the AC-variants comment (`None` renders the trivial-variant
59	/// one-liner). See `rule::render`.
60	pub fn render_rule(r: &ast::Rule, variants: Option<&ast::AcVariants>) -> String {
61	    rule::render(r, variants)
62	}
63	
64	/// R2: one fact (`!Name( a, b )[+]`). See `rule::render_fact`.
65	pub fn render_fact(f: &ast::Fact) -> String {
66	    rule::render_fact(f)
67	}
68	
69	/// R3: one trace formula in top-level (bare) position. See `formula::render`.
70	pub fn render_formula(f: &ast::Formula) -> String {
71	    formula::render(f)
72	}
73	
74	/// R3: one whole restriction block (statement, conditional safety comment,
75	/// expanded-formula comment). See `lemma::render_restriction`.
76	pub fn render_restriction(r: &ast::Restriction) -> String {
77	    lemma::render_restriction(r)
78	}
79	
80	/// R3: one whole lemma block (header, statement, guarded-formula comment,
81	/// proof tail). See `lemma::render_lemma`.
82	pub fn render_lemma(l: &ast::Lemma, guarded: Option<&ast::Guarded>) -> String {
83	    lemma::render_lemma(l, guarded)
84	}

1	//! Open-side adapters: translate live prover values into the clean
2	//! [`crate::producers`] crate's input model.
3	//!
4	//! These functions carry NO render logic — they only map already-computed
5	//! prover values (theory metadata, precomputed wellformedness banner, …) into
6	//! the behavioral input shape the clean fragment producers consume. The bytes
7	//! of every routed fragment are produced by the clean crate; this module is the
8	//! thin value bridge the integration protocol permits.
9	
10	use crate::producers;
11	use crate::state::{TheoryEntry, TheoryOrigin};
12	
13	/// Build the R1 `main/help` pane input from a loaded theory.
14	///
15	/// Mirrors exactly the inputs the ported `help_html` consumed: the theory name,
16	/// the `HH:MM:SS` load time, the `show origin` text (`Local "…"` / `Upload "…"`
17	/// / `Interactive`), and the precomputed `errors_html` wellformedness banner.
18	/// The clean [`producers::render_help_pane`] entity-escapes the name/time/origin
19	/// and appends the shared static help block + `{html,title}` envelope.
20	pub fn help_pane(entry: &TheoryEntry) -> producers::HelpPane {
21	    let origin = match &entry.origin {
22	        TheoryOrigin::Local(p) => format!("Local \"{}\"", p.display()),
23	        TheoryOrigin::Upload(n) => format!("Upload \"{}\"", n),
24	        TheoryOrigin::Interactive => "Interactive".to_string(),
25	    };
26	    producers::HelpPane {
27	        theory_name: entry.name.clone(),
28	        load_time: entry.loaded_at.format("%H:%M:%S").to_string(),
29	        origin,
30	        wf_banner_html: entry.errors_html.clone(),
31	    }
32	}
33

content.content:
1	//! producers-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover
2	//! interactive web UI's fragment PRODUCERS — the code that renders pre-computed
3	//! prover values into the HTML/JSON response-body CONTENT that the already
4	//! clean-roomed dispatch layer serves. Derived purely from black-box
5	//! observation of captured responses + the live oracle. See ../../SPEC.md for
6	//! the observable boundary and the R1–R5 sub-target decomposition, and
7	//! workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the behavioral spec you build up as you probe.
8	//!
9	//! Status: R1 (center section fragments + the shared HTML skin), R5 (the
10	//! theory-path grammar), R2 (the proof-script west pane assembly), R3 (the
11	//! structured proof-tree lines the pane embeds) and R4 (the welcome/index
12	//! page + housekeeping bodies) IMPLEMENTED — `html` (escape / postprocess /
13	//! envelopes), `section` (`render_pane`, `render_help_pane`), `path`
14	//! (`parse` / `render`), `proofscript` (`render_index`, consuming R5 for
15	//! every link and R3 for every proof display), `prooftree` (`render_tree`),
16	//! and `welcome` (`render_welcome` + housekeeping bodies). Gated by
17	//! tests/round1_center_fragments.rs + tests/corpus_sweep.rs (R1: 324-fragment
18	//! byte parity), tests/r5_path_grammar.rs (R5: live-probe acceptance replay +
19	//! 40037-tail corpus byte round-trip), tests/r2_west_pane.rs (R2+R3: all 478
20	//! overview west panes sliced to opaque formula/method text and re-rendered
21	//! byte-identically + live-probe replays), tests/r3_proof_tree.rs (R3
22	//! fixtures + live autoprove replays), and tests/r4_welcome.rs (R4 live-probe
23	//! byte replays).
24	//!
25	//! In scope: pure-render producers — given pre-computed prover values, emit the
26	//! fragment bytes (tags, links, headings, escaping, line breaks, envelope).
27	//! OUT of scope (opaque input / other clusters): the pretty-printed content
28	//! text itself (formula / rule / signature / method text), the constraint
29	//! system and applicable-proof-methods panes, graph DOT/SVG, and the Rust-only
30	//! progressive-UI route.
31	
32	pub mod model;
33	
34	pub mod html; // R1 — shared skin: escaping, line postprocess, JSON envelope
35	pub mod section; // R1 — theory-view center section fragments (round-1 target)
36	pub mod proofscript; // R2 — proof-script west pane (theory index)
37	pub mod prooftree; // R3 — proof-tree / proof-method HTML
38	pub mod welcome; // R4 — welcome/index page + housekeeping
39	pub mod path; // R5 — theory-path grammar (URL <-> structured)
40	
41	pub use model::*;
42	
43	/// Round-1 entry point: render a theory-view center-section content pane
44	/// (`main/message` / `main/rules` / `main/tactic`) to its response-body bytes.
45	/// Delegates to [`section::render_pane`].
46	pub fn render_content_pane(pane: &model::ContentPane) -> String {
47	    section::render_pane(pane)
48	}
49	
50	/// Round-1 entry point: render the `main/help` pane (env line + static help
51	/// block) to its response-body bytes. Delegates to
52	/// [`section::render_help_pane`].
53	pub fn render_help_pane(help: &model::HelpPane) -> String {
54	    section::render_help_pane(help)
55	}
56	
57	/// Round-2 entry point: parse a raw theory-path wildcard tail (the part after
58	/// `/thy/trace/<idx>/main/`, still percent-encoded) into a structured path.
59	/// Delegates to [`path::parse`].
60	pub fn parse_path(raw: &str) -> Option<model::ThyPath> {
61	    path::parse(raw)
62	}
63	
64	/// Round-2 entry point: render a structured theory path to its href segments
65	/// (percent-encoded; join with `/`). Delegates to [`path::render`].
66	pub fn render_path(p: &model::ThyPath) -> Vec<String> {
67	    path::render(p)
68	}
69	
70	/// Round-2 entry point: render the proof-script WEST pane (the theory index
71	/// shown left of every page) to its inner HTML. Delegates to
72	/// [`proofscript::render_index`].
73	pub fn render_proof_script(pane: &model::ProofScriptPane) -> String {
74	    proofscript::render_index(pane)
75	}
76	
77	/// Round-3 entry point: render a lemma's proof tree as the pre-postprocess
78	/// document fragment the west pane embeds (logical lines joined with
79	/// newlines). Delegates to [`prooftree::render_tree`].
80	pub fn render_proof_tree(index: u64, lemma: &str, tree: &model::ProofTree) -> String {
81	    prooftree::render_tree(index, lemma, tree)
82	}
83	
84	/// Round-3 entry point: render the welcome / index (`/`) page body.
85	/// Delegates to [`welcome::render_welcome`].
86	pub fn render_welcome(w: &model::Welcome) -> String {
87	    welcome::render_welcome(w)
88	}
89

1 //! Input model — the pre-computed prover VALUES the producers render. 2 //! 3 //! These are the crate's OWN, minimal, behavior-named types for unit testing 4 //! (build a value with Rust constructors, assert its rendered fragment equals an 5 //! observed capture). At integration the open side wires thin adapters from the 6 //! live typed-theory / proof-state values into this shape; see 7 //! ../../interface/ for the integration surface. 8 //! 9 //! Everything here is a value the producer only RENDERS. The text of a formula, 10 //! rule, signature, or proof method is computed elsewhere (the pretty / solver 11 //! side) and reaches the producer as opaque `content` strings — the producer 12 //! adds the HTML/JSON skin (tags, links, escaping, line breaks, envelope) the 13 //! captures show around it. No field here re-derives prover content. 14 15 // --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 16 // R1 — theory-view CENTER section fragments (`main/
`) 17 // --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 18 19 /// A content pane addressed by a `main/
` route, rendered as the 20 /// `{title, html}` response envelope. The pane is an ordered list of headed 21 /// blocks; the HTML skin (heading tags, monospace paragraph, the per-line 22 /// break/indent postprocess) is the producer's to add. 23 pub struct ContentPane { 24 /// Envelope title (e.g. the message / rules / tactic pane's title text). 25 pub title: String, 26 /// Headed blocks, in document order. 27 pub blocks: Vec, 28 } 29 30 /// One headed block: a heading line plus a monospace body. 31 pub struct HeadedBlock { 32 /// Section heading text (a fixed vocabulary chosen per route). 33 pub heading: String, 34 /// Pre-rendered body content (opaque prover text, already line-structured 35 /// and pre-skinned: entity-escaped with any emphasis spans in place). 36 /// Empty is meaningful. 37 pub body: Content, 38 /// How the block renders when its body is empty (BEHAVIOR.md §4). 39 pub when_empty: EmptyRender, 40 } 41 42 /// Empty-body rendering modes, all three observed (BEHAVIOR.md §4). 43 /// 44 /// The interface header models this as `keep_when_empty: bool`; `Keep`/`Omit` 45 /// map onto true/false and `BlankLine` is the rules pane's macros slot, pinned 46 /// by live probe [L03] (macros present ⇒ the pane starts with `

Macros

`, 47 /// absent ⇒ a single blank line where the block would be — the corpus-wide 48 /// leading `
`). The integration adapter picks the mode per section. 49 pub enum EmptyRender { 50 /// Emit heading + an empty paragraph (tactic; message sections by analogy — 51 /// never observed empty). 52 Keep, 53 /// Drop heading + paragraph but leave one blank line in the document (the 54 /// rules pane's macros slot). 55 BlankLine, 56 /// Vanish without residue (the rules pane's restrictions section). 57 Omit, 58 } 59 60 /// The `main/help` pane input (BEHAVIOR.md §8): the env line's fields plus the 61 /// pre-computed wellformedness banner. The static help block is the producer's 62 /// own fixed content. 63 pub struct HelpPane { 64 pub theory_name: String, 65 /// Load wall-clock time as displayed (`HH:MM:SS`). 66 pub load_time: String, 67 /// Load-origin text (e.g. `Local "/tmp/…/thy/file.spthy"`), un-escaped; 68 /// the producer entity-escapes it. 69 pub origin: String, 70 /// Raw pre-rendered `
…
` block, or empty when 71 /// the theory loaded warning-free (opaque load-time input). 72 pub wf_banner_html: String, 73 } 74 75 /// Opaque pre-rendered content: a sequence of logical lines plus, where known, 76 /// which spans are keyword / operator / comment emphasis (used only for the 77 /// byte-close HTML skin; the acceptance gate compares visible text, not skin). 78 pub struct Content { 79 pub lines: Vec, 80 } 81 82 impl Content { 83 pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool { 84 self.lines.is_empty() || self.lines.iter().all(|l| l.is_empty()) 85 } 86 } 87 88 // --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 89 // R2 — proof-script WEST pane (the theory index shown left of every page) 90 // --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 91 92 /// The whole proof-script pane input. 93 pub struct ProofScriptPane { 94 pub theory_name: String, 95 /// Resolved numeric version index used in every internal link. 96 pub index: u64, 97 /// Top navigation items (message / rules / tactic / sources), in order, 98 /// each with its label and pre-computed annotation. 99 pub items: Vec, 100 pub lemmas: Vec, 101 } 102 103 /// One top-of-index navigation item. 104 /// 105 /// The interface header carries the link target as a `section` string (the 106 /// `main/<…>` wildcard tail); this crate-local model holds it as the R5 107 /// [`ThyPath`] instead so the link is CONSTRUCTED through the theory-path 108 /// grammar — the integration adapter maps the live value via `path::parse`. 109 pub struct NavItem { 110 /// The `main/<…>` route the item links to (rendered via R5). 111 pub target: ThyPath, 112 /// Bold label text (opaque pre-rendered input; the observed vocabulary is 113 /// fixed per slot — BEHAVIOR.md §12). 114 pub label: String, 115 /// Trailing annotation text (a count / cases summary), possibly empty 116 /// (opaque pre-rendered input). 117 pub annotation: String, 118 } 119 120 /// One lemma's index entry: its declaration plus how its proof displays. 121 pub struct LemmaEntry { 122 pub name: String, 123 /// Attribute list text (already assembled by the pretty side), e.g. 124 /// `" [reuse]"`, or empty. May span several logical lines (long heuristic 125 /// lists wrap, with the continuation indent baked into the text). 126 pub attributes: String, 127 /// Trace-quantifier keyword (`all-traces` / `exists-trace`). 128 pub quantifier: String, 129 /// Pre-rendered formula body (opaque prover text, line-structured), at 130 /// indents RELATIVE to the declaration body: the first line carries no 131 /// leading spaces, continuation lines their own deeper indent. The 132 /// renderer adds the 2-space block indent and decides the inline/vertical 133 /// quantifier layout (BEHAVIOR.md §13). 134 pub formula: Content, 135 pub proof: ProofDisplay, 136 } 137 138 /// How a lemma's proof renders in the index. 139 pub enum ProofDisplay { 140 /// Unproven: a single trailing `by sorry` step (no status wrapper). 141 Unproven, 142 /// A structured proof tree, laid out by the R3 renderer 143 /// ([`super::prooftree`]). The lemma HEADER wrapper span is derived from 144 /// the ROOT node's status (a status-less root — an incomplete proof — 145 /// leaves the header unwrapped) [S16][S19]. 146 Tree(ProofTree), 147 } 148 149 // --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 150 // R3 — proof-tree + proof-method HTML 151 // --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 152 153 /// A proof tree: a method-labelled node with named child cases. Pre-computed; 154 /// the producer only lays it out as nested HTML (indent, keywords, links). 155 pub struct ProofTree { 156 /// Pre-rendered proof-method text for this node (opaque prover value; 157 /// may span several lines, continuation indent baked in). 158 pub method_text: String, 159 /// Highlight status this node's step and structural keywords carry. 160 pub status: Highlight, 161 /// Whether this node is part of the real proof (linked step + remove 162 /// affordance) vs a replayed proof-script leftover rendered without links 163 /// [S20]. 164 pub live: bool, 165 /// A terminal MARKER line (the `SOLVED`-style pre-rendered terminal 166 /// state), which never takes the `by ` prefix a case-less method step 167 /// carries [S19]. 168 pub terminal_marker: bool, 169 /// Named child cases, in order; the empty name means a single unnamed 170 /// continuation (no `case` label, URL segment `_`). 171 pub cases: Vec<(String, ProofTree)>, 172 } 173 174 /// The per-step highlight the observed status classes encode. 175 pub enum Highlight { 176 None, 177 Good, 178 Bad, 179 Medium, 180 Replayed, 181 } 182 183 // --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 184 // R4 — welcome / index page + housekeeping 185 // --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 186 187 /// The index (`/`) page input: the loaded-theory table plus a one-shot banner. 188 pub struct Welcome { 189 pub version: String, 190 pub banner: Banner, 191 pub rows: Vec, 192 } 193 194 pub enum Banner { 195 None, 196 Loaded, 197 Failed, 198 Custom(String), 199 } 200 201 /// One row of the loaded-theory table (all fields supplied verbatim). 202 pub struct TheoryRow { 203 pub index: u64, 204 pub name: String, 205 pub time: String, 206 /// A modified (derived) version renders differently from a primary one. 207 pub modified: bool, 208 pub origin: String, 209 } 210 211 // --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 212 // R5 — theory-path grammar (URL <-> structured path) 213 // --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 214 215 /// A theory-internal path parsed from / rendered to the wildcard URL segment 216 /// after `/thy/trace///`. 217 #[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone)] 218 pub enum ThyPath { 219 Help, 220 Message, 221 Rules, 222 Tactic, 223 Sources { refined: bool, source_idx: usize, case_idx: usize }, 224 Lemma(String), 225 Proof { lemma: String, sub: Vec }, 226 Edit(String), 227 Add(String), 228 Delete(String), 229 } 230

=== web_parity.sh head ===
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Web-parity gate: semantic (structural) equivalence of the interactive web
# server between HS (oracle) and the Rust port, across a corpus of theory
# files.  The web analogue of corpus_file_diff.sh.
#
# Per file, two strictly-sequential phases so HS and RS never contend:
#   Phase 1 (HS): boot `HS tamarin-prover interactive` on a temp workdir with
#                 the one theory, crawl it (web_crawl.py), cache the response
#                 manifest content-keyed by sha256(file) under .web_hs_cache/.
#   Phase 2 (RS): boot RS on the same workdir, crawl, diff (web_diff.py) the
#                 two manifests semantically (web_normalize.py) → per-url rows.
#
# Env: FILE_TIMEOUT (per-file cap, 300s), READY_TIMEOUT (server-boot wait, 90s),
#      HS_PORT (3021), RS_PORT (3022), CORPUS_ROOT (tamarin-prover/examples/), ALLOWLIST
#      (one relpath/line; default = seed list below), RESULTS_TSV, MAX_NODES
#      (400), CACHE, HS_PATH, RS_PATH, MAUDE_PATH, TAM_RS_NO_AUTO_BUILD.
# Output TSV (6 col): file  url  status  hs_http  rs_http  kind
#   status ∈ MATCH | DIFF | MISSING_RS | MISSING_HS | SKIP_*
set -u
script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
repo_root="$(cd "$script_dir/.." && pwd)"

FILE_TIMEOUT="${FILE_TIMEOUT:-300}"
READY_TIMEOUT="${READY_TIMEOUT:-90}"
HS_PORT="${HS_PORT:-3021}"
RS_PORT="${RS_PORT:-3022}"
CORPUS_ROOT="${CORPUS_ROOT:-$repo_root/tamarin-prover/examples}"
CACHE="${CACHE:-$script_dir/.web_hs_cache}"
RESULTS_TSV="${RESULTS_TSV:-/tmp/web_parity.tsv}"
MAX_NODES="${MAX_NODES:-400}"

=== web_parity.sh head ===
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Web-parity gate: semantic (structural) equivalence of the interactive web
# server between HS (oracle) and the Rust port, across a corpus of theory
# files.  The web analogue of corpus_file_diff.sh.
#
# Per file, two strictly-sequential phases so HS and RS never contend:
#   Phase 1 (HS): boot `HS tamarin-prover interactive` on a temp workdir with
#                 the one theory, crawl it (web_crawl.py), cache the response
#                 manifest content-keyed by sha256(file) under .web_hs_cache/.
#   Phase 2 (RS): boot RS on the same workdir, crawl, diff (web_diff.py) the
#                 two manifests semantically (web_normalize.py) → per-url rows.
#
# Env: FILE_TIMEOUT (per-file cap, 300s), READY_TIMEOUT (server-boot wait, 90s),
#      HS_PORT (3021), RS_PORT (3022), CORPUS_ROOT (tamarin-prover/examples/), ALLOWLIST
#      (one relpath/line; default = seed list below), RESULTS_TSV, MAX_NODES
#      (400), CACHE, HS_PATH, RS_PATH, MAUDE_PATH, TAM_RS_NO_AUTO_BUILD.
# Output TSV (6 col): file  url  status  hs_http  rs_http  kind
#   status ∈ MATCH | DIFF | MISSING_RS | MISSING_HS | SKIP_*
set -u
script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
repo_root="$(cd "$script_dir/.." && pwd)"

FILE_TIMEOUT="${FILE_TIMEOUT:-300}"
READY_TIMEOUT="${READY_TIMEOUT:-90}"
HS_PORT="${HS_PORT:-3021}"
RS_PORT="${RS_PORT:-3022}"
CORPUS_ROOT="${CORPUS_ROOT:-$repo_root/tamarin-prover/examples}"
CACHE="${CACHE:-$script_dir/.web_hs_cache}"
RESULTS_TSV="${RESULTS_TSV:-/tmp/web_parity.tsv}"
MAX_NODES="${MAX_NODES:-400}"
DIFFDIR="${DIFFDIR:-/tmp/web_parity_diffs}"
mkdir -p "$CACHE"

find_hs_bin() {
    local c
    for c in "$repo_root"/tamarin-prover-testing/.stack-work/install/*/*/*/bin/tamarin-prover; do
        [ -x "$c" ] && { echo "$c"; return 0; }
    done; return 1
}
HS_PATH="${HS_PATH:-$(find_hs_bin)}" || { echo "no HS binary" >&2; exit 2; }
RS_PATH="${RS_PATH:-$repo_root/target/release/tamarin-prover}"
MAUDE_PATH="${MAUDE_PATH:-$(command -v maude)}"

# Auto-build RS (opt out with TAM_RS_NO_AUTO_BUILD=1).
if [ -z "${TAM_RS_NO_AUTO_BUILD:-}" ]; then
    echo "building RS (release)..." >&2
    ( cd "$repo_root" && cargo build --release -q -p tamarin-prover ) || {
        echo "RS build failed" >&2; exit 2; }
fi
[ -x "$RS_PATH" ] || { echo "no RS binary at $RS_PATH" >&2; exit 2; }

# --- file list ---
seed_list() {
    cat <<EOF
Tutorial.spthy
EOF
}
filelist() {
    if [ -n "${ALLOWLIST:-}" ] && [ -f "${ALLOWLIST:-}" ]; then cat "$ALLOWLIST"
    else seed_list; fi

1375	fn render_rule(parsed_rule: &p::Rule, elab: &Theory, macros: &[p::Macro], arity1: &std::collections::HashSet<String>, manual_variants: bool, auto_sources: bool) -> String {
1376	    let name = &parsed_rule.name;
1377	    // Desugar `let x = t in ...` bindings before rendering — HS does
1378	    // this via `applyMacroInProtoRule`/`expandRuleLetBlock` so the
1379	    // emitted rule contains no bound names from the `let` block.
1380	    // Mirrors `apply_let_block` (`elaborate.rs:678`).  HS site:
1381	    // `lib/theory/src/TheoryObject.hs::prettyTheory` → `prettyRule` chain
1382	    // which operates on the post-`applyMacroInProtoRule` rule.
1383	    let desugared = crate::elaborate::apply_let_block(parsed_rule);
1384	    // HS-faithful: an arity-1 function applied with a comma list, `f(a,b,c)`,
1385	    // is folded by `naryOpApp`'s `k == 1` branch into `f(<a,b,c>)`
1386	    // (Theory/Text/Parser/Term.hs:84-87).  RS's term parser keeps the surplus
1387	    // args, so re-fold here before rendering.  See `rewrite_arity1_term`.
1388	    // `arity1` is computed once by the caller and threaded in.
1389	    let premises: Vec<p::Fact> =
1390	        desugared.premises.iter().map(|f| rewrite_arity1_fact(f, arity1)).collect();
1391	    let actions: Vec<p::Fact> =
1392	        desugared.actions.iter().map(|f| rewrite_arity1_fact(f, arity1)).collect();
1393	    let conclusions: Vec<p::Fact> =
1394	        desugared.conclusions.iter().map(|f| rewrite_arity1_fact(f, arity1)).collect();
1395	    // AC-canonicalise the modulo-E body BEFORE handing it to the clean
1396	    // renderer — the parser produces left-associative nested `BinOp` chains for
1397	    // multiset/xor operators, but HS's `fAppAC` flattens+sorts at parse time
1398	    // (a solver-side transform).  The clean crate renders the facts as given,
1399	    // so the canonicalisation stays PORTED here.
1400	    use crate::elaborate::canonicalize_ac_in_pfact;

# Boot a server, wait until it answers on / , run the crawl, then kill it
# (whole process group, to reap maude children).  Args: bin port workdir out.
boot_crawl() {
    local bin="$1" port="$2" wd="$3" out="$4" kind="$5"
    local log="$wd/${kind}_server.log" pid
    # Pin the derivcheck budget like corpus_file_diff.sh does (30s): HS's
    # 5s default expires deterministically on ~12 corpus files even idle,
    # replacing the derivation report with a timeout block RS never emits
    # (48 bogus DIFF rows in the 2026-07-05 sweep).  RS parses the flag on
    # its web path too (hardcoded-5s load is a separate, tracked plumb).
    setsid "$bin" interactive "$wd/thy" --port="$port" \
        --derivcheck-timeout="${DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT:-30}" >"$log" 2>&1 &
    pid=$!
    # wait for readiness
    local ok="" i
    for ((i=0; i<READY_TIMEOUT; i++)); do
        if curl -sf -o /dev/null "http://127.0.0.1:$port/"; then ok=1; break; fi
        kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || break
        sleep 1
    done
    if [ -z "$ok" ]; then
        echo "  $kind server not ready ($wd)" >&2
        kill -- -"$pid" 2>/dev/null; wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        return 1
    fi
    # shellcheck disable=SC2086  # CRAWL_EXTRA_ARGS must word-split
    timeout "$FILE_TIMEOUT" python3 "$script_dir/web_crawl.py" \
        "http://127.0.0.1:$port" "$out" --max-nodes "$MAX_NODES" ${CRAWL_EXTRA_ARGS:-} 2>>"$log"
    local rc=$?
    kill -- -"$pid" 2>/dev/null; wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
    return $rc
}

one_file() {
    local rel="$1" f="$CORPUS_ROOT/$1"
    [ -f "$f" ] || { printf '%s\t-\tSKIP_NO_FILE\t-\t-\t-\n' "$rel"; return 0; }
    # A theory with no lemma declaration legitimately discovers 0 lemmas —
    # allow it; otherwise 0 discovered lemmas is a transient failure and
    # web_crawl.py exits 3 (→ SKIP_*_FAIL below, manifest never cached).
    local CRAWL_EXTRA_ARGS=""
    grep -qE '^[[:space:]]*(lemma|equivLemma|diffLemma)([[:space:]]|\[|:)' "$f" \
        || CRAWL_EXTRA_ARGS="--allow-no-lemmas"
    export CRAWL_EXTRA_ARGS
    local key; key=$(sha256sum "$f" | cut -d' ' -f1)
    local hs_manifest="$CACHE/$key.hs.json"
    local wd; wd=$(mktemp -d)
    mkdir -p "$wd/thy"; cp "$f" "$wd/thy/"
    # Sibling oracle scripts: `heuristic: o "./oracle-…"` resolves relative
    # to the server's theory dir on both engines (upstream's deforacle
    # recipe) — stage them next to the theory or oracle rankings fail.
    local __of
    for __of in "$(dirname "$f")"/oracle*; do
        [ -f "$__of" ] && cp "$__of" "$wd/thy/"
    done

    # Phase 1: HS (cached)
    if [ ! -f "$hs_manifest" ]; then
        if ! MAUDE_PATH="$MAUDE_PATH" boot_crawl "$HS_PATH" "$HS_PORT" "$wd" "$hs_manifest" hs; then
            rm -f "$hs_manifest"; rm -rf "$wd"
            printf '%s\t-\tSKIP_HS_FAIL\t-\t-\t-\n' "$rel"; return 0
        fi
    fi
    # Phase 2: RS
    local rs_manifest="$wd/rs.json"
    if ! boot_crawl "$RS_PATH" "$RS_PORT" "$wd" "$rs_manifest" rs; then
        rm -rf "$wd"
        printf '%s\t-\tSKIP_RS_FAIL\t-\t-\t-\n' "$rel"; return 0
    fi
    # diff
    python3 "$script_dir/web_diff.py" "$hs_manifest" "$rs_manifest" \
        "$wd/parity.tsv" "$DIFFDIR/$rel" >/dev/null 2>&1
    # prefix each row with the file
    awk -F'\t' -v r="$rel" '{print r"\t"$0}' "$wd/parity.tsv"
    rm -rf "$wd"
}

echo "web_parity: HS=$HS_PATH" >&2
echo "web_parity: RS=$RS_PATH  maude=$MAUDE_PATH" >&2
: > "$RESULTS_TSV"
N=$(filelist | grep -c .)
i=0
while IFS= read -r rel; do
    [ -n "$rel" ] || continue
    i=$((i+1)); echo "[$i/$N] $rel" >&2
    one_file "$rel" >> "$RESULTS_TSV"
done < <(filelist | grep .)

echo "=== SUMMARY ===" >&2
awk -F'\t' '{c[$3]++} END{for(k in c) printf "  %-14s %d\n", k, c[k]}' "$RESULTS_TSV" >&2
echo "  files: $N   results: $RESULTS_TSV   diffs: $DIFFDIR" >&2
echo "DONE_WEB_PARITY" >&2

else seed_list; fi
}

echo "web_parity: HS=$HS_PATH" >&2
echo "web_parity: RS=$RS_PATH  maude=$MAUDE_PATH" >&2
: > "$RESULTS_TSV"
N=$(filelist | grep -c .)
i=0
while IFS= read -r rel; do
    [ -n "$rel" ] || continue
    i=$((i+1)); echo "[$i/$N] $rel" >&2
    one_file "$rel" >> "$RESULTS_TSV"
done < <(filelist | grep .)

echo "=== SUMMARY ===" >&2
awk -F'\t' '{c[$3]++} END{for(k in c) printf "  %-14s %d\n", k, c[k]}' "$RESULTS_TSV" >&2
echo "  files: $N   results: $RESULTS_TSV   diffs: $DIFFDIR" >&2
echo "DONE_WEB_PARITY" >&2

2361	fn render_parsed_restriction(r: &p::Restriction, macros: &[p::Macro], predicates: &[p::Predicate], _elab: &Theory, arity1: &std::collections::HashSet<String>) -> String {
2362	    // HS `prettyRestriction` (TheoryObject.hs:846-857):
2363	    //   The `Restriction` carries two formulas after `applyMacroInRestriction`:
2364	    //   - `_rstrFormula`         = macro-EXPANDED formula  (displayed in expanded block)
2365	    //   - `_rstrOriginalFormula` = original macro-form     (displayed on top)
2366	    //   HS always has `ogFormula = Just _` (applyMacroInRestriction sets it
2367	    //   even when there are no macros: `Just $ maybe f id ofm`).
2368	    //
2369	    // RS's `r.formula` is the parser-form (macro calls present).  Apply
2370	    // the theory's macros to get the expanded formula used in the block.
2371	    // Fold arity-1 surplus args into a pair first (HS `naryOpApp` `k == 1`,
2372	    // Term.hs:84-87), exactly as the parser would — applies to BOTH the
2373	    // original and expanded displays since HS folds at parse time.
2374	    //
2375	    // HS `expandRestriction` (TheoryObject.hs:430-437) predicate-expands BOTH
2376	    // formulas (`f'`, `ofm'`), so e.g. the multiset `(<)` operator is rewritten
2377	    // to `∃ z. r = l ++ z` BEFORE the formula is stored — and thus before it is
2378	    // printed.  Mirror that here on both displayed formulas.
2379	    // `arity1` is computed once by the caller and threaded in.
2380	    //
2381	    // HS stores restriction formulas as `LNFormula`, whose AC heads
2382	    // (`Mult`/`Union`/`Xor`/`NatPlus`) are kept in `fAppAC`-sorted order
2383	    // (Term/Term/Raw.hs:118-122) — so a user-written union like `seq1 + dif`
2384	    // displays AC-sorted as `dif++seq1`.  Our parser keeps `BinOp` trees in
2385	    // written order, so re-establish the canonical AC operand order before
2386	    // rendering, exactly as the lemma display path does (render_parsed_lemma,
2387	    // `canonicalize_ac_in_formula`).
2388	    let original = crate::elaborate::canonicalize_ac_in_formula(
2389	        &crate::elaborate::rewrite_arity1_formula(
2390	            &expand_predicates_for_display(&r.formula, predicates), arity1));
2391	    let expanded = if macros.is_empty() {
2392	        original.clone()
2393	    } else {
2394	        crate::elaborate::canonicalize_ac_in_formula(
2395	            &crate::elaborate::rewrite_arity1_formula(
2396	                &expand_predicates_for_display(
2397	                    &crate::macro_expand::apply_macros_formula(macros, &r.formula), predicates),
2398	                arity1))
2399	    };
2400	    // Batch echo (fixed width 110/73, plain text): route the whole restriction
2401	    // block through the vendored clean crate.  The clean `Restriction` carries
2402	    // the statement formula (`original`, macro-form — shown on the top line) and
2403	    // a separate `expanded` field (the macro/predicate-EXPANDED formula shown in
2404	    // the `/* expanded formula: … */` comment); the two differ under macros
2405	    // (witness features/macros/MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions.spthy) and are equal
2406	    // otherwise.  Every solver-entangled transform (predicate expansion, arity-1
2407	    // fold, AC canonicalisation, sort resolution) stays PORTED above; only the
2408	    // already-resolved formulas are handed to the clean crate.  The interactive
2409	    // web source view (narrower width) and message pane (HtmlDoc) keep the ported
2410	    // HughesPJ assembly below — see `batch_echo_width`.
2411	    if batch_echo_width() {
2412	        let stmt = pf::resolve_formula_sorts(&original);
2413	        let exp = pf::resolve_formula_sorts(&expanded);
2414	        return crate::pretty_clean_adapt::restriction_section(&r.name, &stmt, &exp);
2415	    }
2416	    use crate::pretty_hpj::{keyword_, line_comment_, hl_open, hl_close, html_mode,
2417	                            escape_html_entities, Hl};
2418	    let mut out = String::new();
2419	    // HS `kwRestriction <-> text name <> colon` (TheoryObject.hs:848-849):
2420	    // `restriction` is a keyword; the name is `text` (entity-escaped in HtmlDoc
2421	    // mode).  `keyword_`/escaping are identities in plain mode.
2422	    out.push_str(&keyword_("restriction").render());
2423	    out.push(' ');
2424	    if html_mode() { out.push_str(&escape_html_entities(&r.name)); } else { out.push_str(&r.name); }
2425	    out.push_str(":\n");
2426	    // Top-level display: original formula (macro form) — `fromMaybe expandedFormula ogFormula`.
2427	    // Since ogFormula = Just original, this always shows `r.formula` (macro form).
2428	    out.push_str(&pf::formula_doublequoted_nested(&original, 2));
2429	    // Safety annotation: `nest 2 (if safety then lineComment_ "safety formula"
2430	    // else emptyDoc)` (TheoryObject.hs:846-858, see line 851).
